ORDER TO SHOW CAUSE
Off Calendar Sep-02-2005 Continued To Nov-07-05 At 01:30 P.M. In Dept 212

MOTION TO COMPEL ANSWERS TO INTERROGATORIES
Off Calendar Aug-16-2005 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
NO APPEARANCE. TENTATIVE RULING ADOPTED. Plaintiff's motion to compel responses to request for production of documents and special interrogatories is granted. No opposition has been filed. Defendant is ordered to serve complete verified responses without objections to plaintiff's first set of interrogatories and request for production of documents and to produce documents within 15 days of notice of this order. It is further ordered that counsel for the parties meet and confer as to a mutually convenient time to inspect the property. The inspection is to be completed no later than September 30, 2005. Prevailing party to submit form of order. COMMISSIONER: BRUCE E. CHAN

CASE MANAGEMENT CONFERENCE
Off Calendar Jul-12-2005 Set For Order To Show Cause On Sep-06-05 At 01:30 P.M. In Dept 212

Mtn To Compel Responses To And Compliance W/ Pltf'S First Inspection Demand
Off Calendar Jun-22-2005 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
NO APPEARANCE. TENTATIVE RULING ADOPTED. Plaintiff's motion to compel is ordered off calendar as it was not timely noticed. CCP Section 1005, as amended by AB 3078, Chapter 171, Statutes of 2004, requires that motions be served and filed 16 court days prior to the hearing, with five calendar days added if the motion was served by mail. In addition, the motion fails to comply with California Rule of Court 313 as there is no memorandum of points and authorities. Rule 313(b) sets forth the requirements of the memorandum. COMMISSIONER: BRUCE E. CHAN
